description: Pseudo-formula rules for sector state classification. These are starting thresholds, not sacred truths.

shared_metrics.up_down_ratio: number of advancing names / declining names
shared_metrics.leader_count: count of representative leaders behaving well
shared_metrics.breakout_count: count of names breaking above valid base/resistance

WATCH.intent: Sector is improving but not fully confirmed.
WATCH.notes: Use WATCH when the story exists and improvement starts, but conviction is still incomplete.

ACTIVE.intent: Sector has real confirmation from price/volume/breadth.
ACTIVE.notes: ACTIVE means sector deserves real focus. Use this as the main feeder state for candidate filtering.

HOT.intent: Sector is leading strongly and broadly.
HOT.notes: HOT should be harder to trigger than ACTIVE. This is leadership, not just participation.

WEAKENING.intent: Sector is losing confirmation or breaking down.
WEAKENING.notes: Use WEAKENING when sector loses follow-through, not merely because it pauses for one session.

tie_break_notes: If a sector qualifies for both WATCH and WEAKENING, prefer WEAKENING if it was previously ACTIVE/HOT.
If a sector nearly qualifies for HOT but lacks breakout breadth, keep it at ACTIVE.
Macro alignment can support WATCH/ACTIVE but must not override clearly negative real flow.

late_entry_risk_overlay.notes: Do not downgrade the sector automatically, but mark it as extended.
